title: Platform _defaults.yaml — top-level key guard (#658 fast-follow)
description: MINIMAL schema for conf.d _defaults.yaml. The top-level keys are STRICT (additionalProperties:false), so a NON-prefix typo like `state_flters` / `defalts` that would otherwise SILENTLY drop the whole platform-default block (the highest-blast-radius config file — affects every tenant under that directory) fails at author/CI time. SCOPE LIMIT: prefix-class keys (`_state_*` / `_routing*`) are accepted via patternProperties to mirror the tenant validator, so a prefix-INTERNAL typo (e.g. `_routing_defualts`) is NOT caught here — same lenient prefix model as ValidateTenantKeys. NESTED values are intentionally LEFT LOOSE ({} = accept anything): the keys under `defaults` (metric names) and `state_filters` (filter names) are dynamic and platform-authored, so they are NOT modelled here (a full structural schema is the deferred #658 item (a)). This is the cheap, high-value middle ground between 'no validation' and 'full JSON Schema'.
$comment: Top-level properties mirror what the Go loader (pkg/config/types.go ThresholdConfig) reads from ANY conf.d file: the platform-default blocks (defaults / state_filters / _routing_defaults) PLUS the struct's other top-level fields (tenants / profiles / max_metrics_per_tenant) — Go yaml.Unmarshal has no KnownFields, so these are loader-legitimate in a _defaults.yaml and must NOT false-reject (e.g. a platform-wide max_metrics_per_tenant cap, which has no other `_*` file home). The `_*` properties additionally mirror _lib_constants.py VALID_RESERVED_KEYS (the 3-way reserved-key SSOT); tests/lint/test_check_confd_schema.py asserts this set stays a superset of VALID_RESERVED_KEYS so a newly added reserved key can't silently make this guard false-reject a legitimate inherited override. The `^_state_` / `^_routing` patternProperties mirror VALID_RESERVED_PREFIXES.

defaults.title: Platform default thresholds (metric → value)
defaults.description: Map of metric key → default threshold (scalar or scheduled). Keys are dynamic metric names → values left loose; only the top-level `defaults` key itself is guarded. `null`/empty is tolerated — Go unmarshals `defaults: null` (or `defaults:`) into a nil map (a valid empty-defaults placeholder); rejecting it would be Schema-red-but-system-runs friction (Gemini #913 對抗1).

state_filters.title: Platform default state filters
state_filters.description: Map of filter-name → {reasons, severity, default_state}. Filter names are dynamic → values left loose. `null`/empty tolerated for the same nil-map reason as `defaults`.

tenants.title: ThresholdConfig.tenants — loader-legitimate top-level field (left loose; guarded by tenant-config.schema.json when in a tenant file)

profiles.title: ThresholdConfig.profiles — loader-legitimate top-level field (profile map; usually in _routing_profiles.yaml but the loader reads it from any file)

max_metrics_per_tenant.title: ThresholdConfig.max_metrics_per_tenant — platform-wide cardinality cap; loader reads it from any file, no other `_*` home

_routing_defaults.title: Default routing inherited by all tenants under this directory
